extraction.one_line_summary: GAVIRIA Fernando won Stage 1 sprint with Quick-Step controlling front from race start through 200m, gapping SAGAN by 3 bike lengths
tactical_patterns: Quick-Step deployed 5-man leadout with 1.5km remaining on technical finish, preventing positional challenges from Bora-Hansgrohe entirely.
Gaviria captured 26-point lead in green jersey by winning intermediate sprint 4th place, devastating Sagan's accumulation strategy early.
Froome lost 1:01 in sub-5km crash, revealing Sky vulnerability in technical finishes despite neutralization protocol expectations.

rider_signals.GAVIRIA Fernando: positive: Track omnium background enables superior bike handling on technical finishes compared to pure power sprinters like Kittel
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: negative: Bora-Hansgrohe positioning inferior to Quick-Step despite Sagan's finishing speed, 26-point deficit catastrophic for green jersey
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: negative: Lost 1:01 on Stage 1 crash, heavy impact suggests positioning errors uncharacteristic for experienced GC leader
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: negative: Lost time in finish despite strong 2017 Tour, suggests preparation or positioning deficiencies from previous year
